feedingProfile.preferredNpk: 15.5-0-0 (calcium nitrate) or balanced; side-dress 3-4 weeks after planting

commonIssues: Bolts (sends up seed stalk and turns bitter) when soil dries out or once days warm toward 27C (80F) and daylength lengthens; grow in spring/fall and keep evenly moist.
Heat above 29C (85F) soil inhibits seed germination; optimum germination soil temp is about 21C (70F).
Premature flowering from inconsistent watering, especially in containers which dry quickly and need more frequent watering.

Container minimum (~10 L) reflects spinach's shallow root system; a wider, shallower pot (at least 30 cm / 1 ft wide and deep per RHS) suits baby-leaf and cut-and-come-again harvests better than a deep narrow one.
Fungal disease and slug/snail pressure increase with overhead/evening watering; water the soil in the morning.

source.citation: Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC, Spinach factsheet: days to maturity 37-45 days; optimum germination soil temp 70F; >85F inhibits germination; survives 20F without injury; side-dress 1 lb calcium nitrate (15.5-0-0) per 100 sq ft 3-4 weeks after planting; soil pH 6.0-7.0; spring (Mar-Apr Piedmont, Feb-Apr Coastal) and fall (Aug-Oct) planting.

Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC, Spinach factsheet: fertilizer side-dress rate and timing (calcium nitrate 15.5-0-0, 3-4 weeks after planting); soil pH 6.0-7.0; moisten soil to 6 inches; water in morning.

UMN Extension, Growing spinach and Swiss chard in home gardens: supply about one inch of water (rain) per week; water more frequently on sandy soil; spacing two to four inches apart; sow spring crops until temperatures reach 80F; fall crops sown ~2 months before first frost will not bolt under shortening days.

Penn State Extension, Growing Spinach (A Cool-Season Vegetable): optimum growing temperature 50-60F; seeds germinate best 45-68F; young seedlings survive as low as 15-20F; full sun but tolerates partial shade; keep soil consistently moist; dry conditions trigger bolting; grows in containers with adequate size and nutrient-rich soilless potting mix.
Penn State Extension, Growing Spinach: optimum growing temperature 50-60F (~10-16C); young seedlings tolerate 15-20F (~-9 to -7C); spacing four to six inches.

RHS, How to grow spinach: choose a sunny or lightly shaded site; thirsty crop, needs watering in dry weather; plant 15cm (6in) apart, thin to 7.5cm (3in); sow mid-to-late spring and late summer to early autumn; containers at least 30cm (1ft) wide and deep, potting compost dries quickly and needs frequent watering; optimal growth 15-20C (60-70F); baby leaves in ~1 month, mature in ~2 months.
RHS, How to grow spinach: sunny or lightly shaded planting site; partial shade beneficial in warmer months to reduce heat stress and bolting.

The Old Farmer's Almanac, Spinach (Planting, Growing, and Harvesting): cool-season crop; can survive frost and temps down to 15F (-9C); bolts as days lengthen (~14h) and temperatures reach 80F; supply 1-2 inches water per week; harvest ~37-45 days after planting.

Confidence note: wateringProfile.baseIntervalDays (2) is estimated from extension guidance of ~1 inch/week with the caveat that spinach is a thirsty crop that bolts if the soil dries out and that container compost dries quickly (RHS, PSU, UMN); no source gives an explicit day-interval, so a conservative 2-day interval is used for containers/warm conditions. hotWeatherMultiplier (1.5) and rainSkipThresholdMm (25 ~= 1 inch/week) are derived, not directly cited.

Confidence note: containerProfile liters are converted/estimated from RHS 'at least 30cm wide and deep' (~27 L for a 30cm cube) and PSU 'adequately sized container' for a shallow-rooted green; recommendedMinLiters (10) is a conservative practical minimum for a small baby-leaf pot, idealMin/Max (18-27) bracket the RHS 30cm guidance. No source states liters directly.

Confidence note: feedingProfile.baseIntervalDays (21) generalized from Clemson's single side-dress at 3-4 weeks after planting; spinach is a short-season crop usually fed once or twice rather than on a fixed recurring interval. containerLeachAdjustment (0.5) is a derived estimate for faster nutrient leaching in pots, not directly cited.
